sourceMovie.title: The Ghost Writer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Ghost Writer (2010) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ghost Writer clicked for you, The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Ghost Writer?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Ultimatum, Murder on the Orient Express, Three Days of the Condor—each shares DNA with The Ghost Writer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Ghost Writer?
faq.answer: The Ghost Writer (2010) is best described as Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Ghost Writer worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
